title: Other Missiles in News
focus: Key features, ranges, and developers of various tactical missiles including BrahMos, Astra, Pralay, Akash Prime, ULPGM-V3, and Rampage.
rationale: This section covers a distinct cluster of discrete missile systems, their technical specifications, and origins, making it ideal for comparative or specific factual questions.
sectionExcerpt: BrahMos Missile: Jointly developed by India and Russia. A long-range supersonic cruise missile... Astra Missile: Indigenous Beyond Visual Range Air-to-Air missile (BVRAAM)... Pralay missile: A surface-to-surface short range solid propellant quasi-ballistic missile... Rampage Missiles: Long range, air to ground precise attack missile... Developed by: Israel Aerospace Industries.
evidenceBullets: BrahMos is a 2-stage supersonic cruise missile (solid booster, liquid ramjet) jointly developed by India and Russia with a range up to 290 km.
Astra is an indigenous Beyond Visual Range Air-to-Air missile (BVRAAM) developed by DRDO with a range exceeding 100 km.
Pralay is a surface-to-surface short-range quasi-ballistic missile (150-500 km) capable of hypersonic speeds.
Akash Prime is a rugged SAM system designed for extreme cold and high altitudes above 4500 meters.
Rampage is an Israeli-developed long-range, air-to-ground supersonic precise attack missile.

title: Scramjet Engine and Hypersonic Weapons
focus: Mechanics, advantages, and differences between Ramjet and Scramjet engines, along with the classification of Hypersonic weapons.
rationale: The text shifts from specific missile systems to the underlying propulsion technologies (air-breathing engines) and the broader category of hypersonic weapons they enable.
sectionExcerpt: A scramjet engine means a Supersonic Combusting Ramjet engine. It is an improvement over the ramjet engine... Ramjet Engine: Mach 3 to 6, Subsonic Combustion. Scramjet Engine: Above Mach 6, Supersonic Combustion... Hypersonic Weapon: Travel at Mach-5 or faster... Types: Hypersonic Glide Vehicles (HGV)... Hypersonic Cruise Missiles (HCM).
evidenceBullets: Ramjet engines operate at Mach 3 to 6 with subsonic combustion, while Scramjet engines operate above Mach 6 with supersonic combustion.
Both Ramjets and Scramjets require assisted take-off (e.g., by a rocket) as they cannot produce thrust at zero airspeed.
Scramjets are more efficient than rockets because they use atmospheric oxygen for combustion instead of carrying an oxidizer.
Hypersonic weapons travel at Mach 5 or faster and fly at lower altitudes than ballistic missiles, making them harder to track.
Hypersonic Glide Vehicles (HGV) are launched from rockets and glide to targets, whereas Hypersonic Cruise Missiles (HCM) are powered throughout flight by Scramjet engines.

title: GSAT-7R (CMS-03) Satellite
focus: Launch, orbit, coverage, and strategic purpose of the GSAT-7R defence communication satellite.
rationale: This is a distinct shift from missiles and propulsion to space-based defence communication infrastructure.
sectionExcerpt: GSAT-7R (CMS-03) Satellite was successfully launched aboard the ISRO's LVM3... Launched into the Geosynchronous Transfer Orbit (GTO), it will eventually settle into Geostationary Orbit... Lifespan: 15 years and will replace the multi-band communication satellite GSAT-7/INSAT-4F Rukmini. Purpose: Strengthens space-based communication, maritime domain awareness, and secure links among Navy ships...
evidenceBullets: GSAT-7R (CMS-03) was launched aboard ISRO's LVM3 into a Geosynchronous Transfer Orbit (GTO) to eventually settle in Geostationary Orbit.
It provides multi-band telecommunication services across the Indian Ocean Region up to 2,000 km from India's coastline.
It has a 15-year lifespan and replaces the GSAT-7/INSAT-4F Rukmini satellite.
Its primary purpose is to strengthen maritime domain awareness and secure communication links for the Indian Navy.
